info.title: Social Network API
info.description: HTTP contract for the Go backend and React JavaScript frontend. Posting supports privacy, nested comments, JPEG/PNG/GIF media, mutually exclusive like/dislike votes, author edits, soft-deletion tombstones, groups, events, chat, notifications, and WebSocket delivery.

tags.name: Chats
tags.description: Direct messages and group chat rooms

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Groups, membership requests, invitations, and group events

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: Follow, group, event, and other user notifications

tags.name: Realtime
tags.description: WebSocket connection for live messages and notifications

Response.description: Standard API response envelope. Successful responses contain a payload in `data` and no field errors. Error responses contain no payload and may provide field-specific messages in `errors`.

status.description: Whether the request succeeded or failed.

message.description: A human-readable summary of the result.

data.description: The response payload. Error responses use null, an empty object, or an empty array.

errors.description: Field-specific validation messages. Successful responses use null or an empty object.

PrivateUser.description: User details that are visible only to the authenticated owner.

id.description: The group ID or generated direct-message thread ID.

name.description: The group title or the other participant's display name.

last_message.description: A preview of the most recent message.

updated_at.description: When the conversation last received activity.

ChatRoom.description: A direct-message or group conversation visible to the current user.

examples.name: Amina Njeri
examples.last_message: Hey, are you joining the walk this weekend?

VoteRequest.description: The vote the current user wants to set on a post or comment.

avatar.description: URL of the user's avatar, or null when none is set.

PublicUser.description: The public identity fields used when embedding a user in another resource.

CurrentUser.description: The authenticated user's public identity and private profile fields.

posts.description: Posts authored by the user that are visible to the requester, in feed order.

followers.description: Users following this profile who are visible to the requester.

following.description: Users followed by this profile who are visible to the requester.

UserProfile.description: A user's complete profile, including signup information, authored posts, followers, and followed users, returned when the requester is allowed to view it.

posts.content: First hike of the season was worth the early alarm.

ViewerVote.description: The current user's vote on the resource, or `none` when no vote exists.

deleted.description: Always false for an active post.

author.description: The user who created the post.

group_id.description: The owning group, or null for a profile or home-feed post.

image_url.description: URL of the attached image or GIF, or null when absent.

privacy.description: Ignored for authorization when group_id is present; accepted group membership is then required.

comment_count.description: Number of non-deleted comments visible in the thread.

viewer_vote.description: The current user's vote on this post.

updated_at.description: When the post was last edited, or null when never edited.

Post.description: A visible, active post with author, audience, media, counts, and viewer state.

examples.content: First hike of the season was worth the early alarm.

deleted.description: Always true for a deleted-post tombstone.

DeletedPost.description: The minimal tombstone returned in place of a soft-deleted post.

deleted.description: Always false for an active comment.

parent_comment_id.description: The parent comment, or null for a top-level comment.

author.description: The user who created the comment.

image_url.description: URL of the attached image or GIF, or null when absent.

viewer_vote.description: The current user's vote on this comment.

updated_at.description: When the comment was last edited, or null when never edited.

replies.description: Visible direct replies, recursively including their descendants.

Comment.description: A visible, active comment with recursively nested replies.

deleted.description: Always true for a deleted-comment tombstone.

replies.description: Visible replies retained beneath the tombstone.

DeletedComment.description: A minimal comment tombstone that preserves any visible descendants.

VoteResponse.description: Updated vote totals and the current user's resulting vote.

PostResponse.description: A successful response containing an active post or a deleted-post tombstone.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

data.content: First hike of the season was worth the early alarm.

CommentResponse.description: A successful response containing an active comment or a deleted-comment tombstone.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

PostListResponse.description: A paginated collection of visible posts and deleted-post tombstones.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of results.

data.content: First hike of the season was worth the early alarm.

CommentListResponse.description: A paginated collection of top-level comments, each containing its nested replies.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of results.

limit.description: Maximum number of top-level resources requested.

Pagination.description: Offset-based pagination metadata for a collection response.

id.description: The group unique identifier from the groups table.

creator_id.description: The user that created the group, or null if that account was deleted.

description.description: The group description or null when none was supplied.

Group.description: A social group record. The fields mirror the groups table in schema.dbml.

examples.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
examples.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

GroupListResponse.description: A paginated response envelope containing groups visible to the authenticated user.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

data.description: Groups ordered by recent creation or server-defined browse ranking.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of groups.

data.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
data.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

id.description: The event unique identifier from the events table.

group_id.description: The group that owns this event.

creator_id.description: The user that created the event, or null if that account was deleted.

description.description: The event description or null when none was supplied.

event_date.description: When the event is scheduled to happen.

created_at.description: When the event record was created.

Event.description: A group event record. The fields mirror the events table in schema.dbml.

examples.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
examples.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

EventListResponse.description: A paginated response envelope containing events for a group.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of events.

data.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
data.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

id.description: The message unique identifier from the messages table.

sender_id.description: The user that sent the message.

dm_thread_id.description: The direct-message thread for a DM message, or null for a group message.

group_id.description: The group for a group chat message, or null for a direct message.

Message.description: A direct-message or group-message record. Exactly one of dm_thread_id or group_id is set.

examples.content: Hey, are you joining the walk this weekend?

MessageListResponse.description: A paginated response envelope containing chat messages ordered oldest to newest for rendering history.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of messages.

data.content: Hey, are you joining the walk this weekend?

id.description: The notification unique identifier from the notifications table.

user_id.description: The user who receives the notification.

type.description: The notification type stored in schema.dbml.

source_id.description: The source entity for this notification. Its meaning depends on the type.

is_read.description: Whether the recipient has marked this notification as read.

Notification.description: A notification record for follow requests, group invitations, event invitations, group join requests, and created events.

NotificationListResponse.description: A paginated response envelope containing notifications for the authenticated user.

status.description: Indicates that the request completed successfully.

pagination.description: Pagination state for this page of notifications.

ResourceId.name: id
ResourceId.description: Unique identifier of the resource addressed by this endpoint.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of top-level resources to return.

Offset.name: offset
Offset.description: Number of top-level resources to skip before collecting results.

Forbidden.description: The resource exists but the current user may not access it

Conflict.description: The requested operation conflicts with current resource state

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by name, nickname, or email

get.description: Returns users visible to the current user, optionally filtered by the search query.
Returns the selected user's complete profile when it is public, owned by the requester, or visible through an accepted follow relationship.

get.summary: Get all comments made by the authenticated user
get.description: Returns comments authored by the current user using offset pagination.
Returns users who follow the selected user and are visible to the requester.

get.summary: Get users followed by this user
get.description: Returns users followed by the selected user and visible to the requester.

post.description: Follows a public profile immediately or creates a pending request for a private profile.

delete.summary: Unfollow a user or cancel follow request
delete.description: Removes an accepted follow relationship or cancels the current user's pending request.

get.description: Returns a list of all DMs and Group chats the user is part of, sorted by recent activity.

post.summary: Open a direct message with a user
post.description: Creates or retrieves an existing DM thread with the target user.

post.summary: Invite a user to a group
post.description: Invites an eligible user to join the selected group.

post.summary: Invite a user to an event
post.description: Invites an eligible user to attend the selected event.
Creates a user account from the submitted profile and credentials.
Authenticates credentials and creates the session cookie used by protected endpoints.
Invalidates the current session and clears its cookie.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's complete profile and account settings.

current_password.description: Required when changing the email address or password.

nickname.description: The optional username or display name.

avatar.description: Replacement JPEG, PNG, or GIF avatar.

remove_avatar.description: Removes the current avatar when true. Cannot be combined with avatar.

patch.description: Updates any supplied account or profile fields. At least one field is required; changing email or password requires the current password.

password.description: The current password used to confirm permanent account deletion.

200.description: Account deleted and session cookie cleared

delete.description: Permanently deletes the account in one transaction. Authored posts and comments are soft-deleted first and remain as tombstones; memberships, follows, sessions, votes, invitations, direct-message threads, messages, and notifications are removed. Groups and events created by the user remain without a creator.

get.summary: Get posts visible on a user's profile
get.description: Returns posts the current user may view on the selected user's profile.

get.summary: Get the home feed or a group feed
get.description: Without group_id, excludes group posts and applies ordinary privacy. With group_id, requires accepted membership and returns only that group's posts.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: When supplied, returns the selected group's feed and requires accepted membership.

audience_ids.description: Repeat the audience_ids form field for each selected accepted follower.

image.description: Optional JPEG, PNG, or GIF up to 5 MiB.

post.description: Creates a profile, follower-only, selected-follower, or group post with optional media.

get.summary: Get a visible post or its tombstone
get.description: Returns the post when visible, using a minimal tombstone if it has been soft-deleted.

patch.summary: Edit an active post as its author
patch.description: Updates the mutable content, media, and audience fields of the author's active post.

delete.summary: Soft-delete a post as its author
delete.description: Idempotent. Comments remain readable to users authorized by the retained post visibility data, but the deleted post accepts no new comments or votes.

get.summary: Get paginated top-level comments with all descendants
get.description: Includes minimal comment tombstones. Authorized users may read historical comments after post deletion.

post.summary: Create a comment or reply on an active post

409.description: Post or selected parent comment is deleted

post.description: Creates a top-level comment or reply on an active post, with optional media.

put.summary: Set or switch the current user's post vote
put.description: Creates or replaces the current user's mutually exclusive like or dislike vote.

delete.summary: Remove the current user's post vote
delete.description: Removes the current user's vote and returns the updated totals.

patch.summary: Edit an active comment as its author
patch.description: Updates the text or media of the author's active comment.

delete.description: Allowed for the comment author or parent post author. Idempotent and preserves descendants.

put.summary: Set or switch the current user's comment vote
put.description: Creates or replaces the current user's mutually exclusive like or dislike vote.

delete.summary: Remove the current user's comment vote
delete.description: Removes the current user's vote and returns the updated totals.

get.description: Returns groups available to the authenticated user for discovery. The response uses offset pagination and returns records that align with the groups table: id, creator_id, title, description, and created_at.

data.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
data.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

post.description: Creates a new group owned by the authenticated user and adds that user as an accepted group member. Title is required; description may be null or an empty string depending on frontend UX.

value.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
value.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

value.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
value.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

get.description: Returns a single group by id. Use this endpoint when opening a group page before fetching its posts, events, members, or chat history.

value.title: Nairobi Weekend Hikers
value.description: Planning easy hikes and photo walks around Nairobi.

post.description: Creates a pending_request row in group_members for the authenticated user. If the user already has an invite, the backend may accept the membership immediately instead of creating a second pending row.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The member or pending member to update.

patch.summary: Resolve a group membership request or invite
patch.description: Allows a group creator to accept or reject a pending_request, and allows an invited user to accept or decline their own pending_invite. Accepted memberships use the accepted status from schema.dbml; rejected or declined memberships are removed.

action.description: accept stores status accepted; reject removes the pending membership row.

delete.description: Removes a user from group_members. Group owners or admins use this to kick a member out; a backend may also allow the authenticated user to leave by targeting their own user_id.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: The group that owns the events.

get.description: Returns events for a group visible to the authenticated user. Event records align with the events table and are usually ordered by upcoming event_date first.

data.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
data.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

post.description: Creates an event within a group. The authenticated user must be an accepted group member. The created event uses the group_id path parameter and the authenticated user as creator_id.

value.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
value.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

value.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
value.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

get.description: Returns a single event by id. The requester must be allowed to view the owning group.

value.title: Karura Forest Saturday Walk
value.description: Meet at Gate A, bring water and comfortable shoes.

post.description: Creates or updates the authenticated user's row in event_rsvps. Only accepted group members may RSVP, and the status must be going or not_going.

get.description: Returns paginated message history for a direct-message thread or group chat room. For DM rooms, id is the dm_threads.id. For group chats, id is the groups.id. Only participants may read history.

data.content: Hey, are you joining the walk this weekend?

get.description: Upgrades an authenticated HTTP request to a WebSocket connection. The session_token cookie authenticates the user. Client-to-server payloads use JSON, for example `{ "type": "message.send", "chat_id": "f9b81580-91f1-42ef-b0c9-f9170587d57c", "content": "Hello" }`. Server-to-client message events look like `{ "type": "message.created", "data": <Message> }`; notification events look like `{ "type": "notification.created", "data": <Notification> }`; read receipts may use `{ "type": "notification.read", "data": { "id": "b4dfcf9c-d761-48a2-9851-8b79974c9e20" } }`. REST endpoints remain the source of truth for pagination and recovery after reconnects.

Returns the authenticated user's notifications ordered newest first. Each notification mirrors the notifications table and uses source_id to point at the related user, group, event, or request source.

patch.description: Sets is_read to true for one notification owned by the authenticated user and returns the updated notification record.

parameters.name: follower_id
parameters.description: The user who follows the authenticated user or sent a pending follow request.

patch.description: Accepts a pending follower request by changing the followers row from pending to accepted. The authenticated user is the followee; follower_id identifies the requester.

delete.summary: Decline a follow request or remove a follower
delete.description: Deletes the followers row where follower_id is the requester/follower and the authenticated user is the followee. This declines pending requests and also removes an already accepted follower.
